title: glycine max nothosubsp. gracilis
intro: Glycine max nothosubsp. gracilis, also known as Adzuki bean or red mung bean, is a variant of the soybean plant that is primarily cultivated for its edible beans and seeds. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high levels of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of consuming Glycine max nothosubsp. gracilis include reducing the risk of heart disease, improving digestion, and supporting bone health, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the seeds and beans of this plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and supplements that are designed to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
